hero.title: Build a portfolio without code, then export HTML you own.
hero.description: A focused editor for developers, designers, and artists who want a portfolio in a day. Keep editing locally, export anytime, and deploy wherever you choose.

highlights.title: No-code portfolio editor
highlights.description: Write the story, not the setup. Fill in your profile, projects, and sections from a clean UI.

highlights.title: Local save, always
highlights.description: Your work persists in the browser. Close it today, continue tomorrow, no login required.

highlights.title: Export HTML you own
highlights.description: Download your index.html, then follow the in-app guide page for GitHub Pages, Vercel, and Netlify deployment.

how.title: How it works
how.description: The editor is built around structured data. Templates render what you write, so your portfolio stays portable.

steps: Pick a template made for your role.
Edit data in a structured, guided form.
Export and deploy anywhere you want.

focus.title: Built for focus
focus.description: No auto-deploy, no lock-in. You own the export and decide where it lives.

mvp.title: MVP priorities
mvp.description: Editor quality and export fidelity first. Logins, cloud save, and deployment integrations come later.

templates.label: Template Gallery
templates.title: Pick a template that fits your story.
templates.description: Every template is designed to export a clean HTML file you fully own. Start with the layout that matches your role, then customize inside the editor.

devPro.title: Developer Basics
devPro.summary: A baseline developer portfolio with intro, stack, and projects.

artistGrid.title: Artist Grid
artistGrid.summary: Visual grid with hero statement and featured works.

studioClassic.title: Studio Classic
studioClassic.summary: Timeline + case studies for studios and small teams.

minimalZen.title: Minimal Zen
minimalZen.summary: A calm, type-first layout that keeps the story tight.

boldStatement.title: Bold Statement
boldStatement.summary: High-contrast hero with oversized headings and punchy copy.

productCasebook.title: Product Casebook
productCasebook.summary: Case-study focused layout for product teams and studios.

gridline.title: Gridline
gridline.summary: Structured grid for work snapshots and role highlights.

monoPortfolio.title: Mono Portfolio
monoPortfolio.summary: Monochrome layout with sections tuned for developers.

atelierPortfolio.title: Atelier Portfolio
atelierPortfolio.summary: Editorial layout that centers visuals and captions.

editor.label: Editor
editor.title: Build your portfolio in one flow.
editor.description: Fill in the structured fields on the left. Preview updates instantly on the right.
editor.sectionsDescription: Reorder and edit each section. Saving happens automatically.
editor.previewHeadline: Short headline about what you build and who you help.

guide.label: Deployment Guide
guide.title: How to deploy your exported HTML
guide.description: Deploy your portfolio for free on GitHub Pages, Vercel, or Netlify. Follow the platform-specific steps to go live in minutes.

intro.title: Before you start
intro.description: The HTML file you export from the editor is fully self-contained. You can host it anywhere. The guides below cover the most popular free deployment platforms.

github.title: GitHub Pages
github.description: Host static sites directly from your GitHub repository.
steps: Create a new GitHub repository (e.g., my-portfolio).
Commit the exported index.html file to the repository root.
Go to Settings > Pages and select 'Deploy from a branch'.
Choose the main branch and / (root) folder, then click Save.
Your site will be live at https://[username].github.io/[repository] in a few minutes.

vercel.title: Vercel
vercel.description: Platform with fast deployment and automatic HTTPS.
steps: Click New Project and connect a GitHub repo or upload files directly.
Leave Framework Preset as 'Other' and click Deploy.
Once deployed, you'll get an automatic HTTPS URL.
To add a custom domain, go to Project Settings > Domains.

netlify.title: Netlify
steps: On the Sites page, select 'Add new site' > 'Deploy manually'.
Drag and drop your exported HTML file.
Deployment starts automatically and you'll get a URL in seconds.
In Site settings, you can change the site name or add a custom domain.

tips.title: Deployment tips
items: All platforms provide automatic HTTPS on free plans.
To connect a custom domain, you'll need to configure DNS settings (see platform-specific guides).
After editing your HTML, simply commit or re-upload the file to redeploy automatically.
GitHub Pages requires your repository to be public for free hosting.

cta.title: Ready to go?
cta.description: Head back to the editor to complete your portfolio and export the HTML.
